title: Smoky Silky Japanese Bacon Ramen
description: A low-sodium, fermented-inspired ramen featuring crispy bacon lardons, silky broth, and a fragrant tadka.
instructions: Cook the ramen noodles in a pot of boiling water for 3–4 minutes until al dente; drain and set aside.
In a small skillet, sauté the bacon lardons over medium heat until crisp; transfer to a plate and reserve the drippings.
Deglaze the skillet with a splash of water, scraping up browned bits, and pour into a separate pot.
Add the dashi stock, low-sodium soy sauce, tamari, miso paste, and remaining water to the pot; bring to a gentle simmer.
Return the bacon lardons to the broth and simmer for 3 minutes to meld flavors.
In another saucepan, heat a teaspoon of oil, drop cumin, mustard, and chili flakes, then add minced garlic; sauté for 20–30 seconds until fragrant—this is your tadka.
Pour the tadka into the simmering broth, stirring to combine.
Add the cooked ramen noodles to the soup and heat through for 1 minute.
Transfer the ramen to bowls, ensuring each has an even portion of broth and bacon.
Garnish with chopped scallions and a drizzle of bacon drippings from the skillet.
Serve immediately, optionally adding a splash of rice vinegar for extra tang if desired.
